gaps: asr: engine invariants R2/R3/R4/KV-overflow not observable on 'trt_edgellm' (qwen3-asr edgellm runtime is v0.8.0-only); corpus transcript baseline captured instead
tts/customvoice: BLOCKED — see blocked_dimensions_investigation (0.7.1-vs-0.7.0 worker runtime mismatch + unified-memory contention with edge-llm-chat-service).
tts/moss: BLOCKED — unified-memory contention (MOSS sm87 engines ARE on HF; not attempted live).
n2: BLOCKED — depends on a coherent qwen3-tts worker; --max_slots-drop overlay prepared but synthesis blocked by the two blockers above.

blocked_dimensions_investigation.summary: qwen3-tts/CustomVoice, MOSS, and N=2 remain BLOCKED on orin-nx. Two compounding blockers found; neither fixable within task scope (no engine/worker rebuild, cannot stop co-resident edge-llm-chat-service).

blocker_1_runtime_version_mismatch.detail: CustomVoice engines are model version 0.7.1; the cached image's bundled qwen3_tts_worker AND the ~/project/repro-qwen3 build (TensorRT-Edge-LLM build/examples/omni/qwen3_tts_worker, May 25, branch qwen3-tts-highperf-runtime-w8a16) are BOTH runtime version 0.7.0.
blocker_1_runtime_version_mismatch.evidence: worker log: 'Model version 0.7.1 does not match runtime version 0.7.0. Consider re-exporting or re-building'; strings on both worker binaries report 0.7.0.
blocker_1_runtime_version_mismatch.impact: warning-level at load, but /tts/stream returns only the 4-byte SR header (no PCM); worker 'exited during stream'.

blocker_2_unified_memory_contention.detail: edge-llm-chat-service (a TRT-LLM serving container, out-of-scope to stop) holds ~10GB of Jetson unified GPU memory (invisible to ps/docker-stats RSS). tegrastats RAM 13712/15656MB with only seeed-voice stopped. After bringing up the baseline TTS service, available RAM fell to ~1.8GB.
blocker_2_unified_memory_contention.evidence: qwen3_tts_worker reaches 'worker_init_error' / 'worker exited during stream' with mem_available_mb dropping 3366->773 and 857->233 during engine deserialization + code2wav activation; TensorRT 'weight streaming enabled will increase peak CPU memory usage by 1X weights size'. FP16 talker (904MB) reached worker_after_ready+stateful_code2wav then died at synthesis; w8a16 talker (456MB) sometimes init-errored, sometimes reached ready but produced 0 audio.
blocker_2_unified_memory_contention.qwen3_tts_engine_footprint: talker-w8a16 456MB + code_predictor(llm.engine) 191MB + code2wav 234MB ~= 881MB engines, ~1.7GB peak with weight-streaming 1x duplication + activations, on top of resident qwen3_asr_worker (~2GB).

root_cause_rediagnosis_vs_task_premise.task_premise: backend passes --max_slots which the OLD bundled worker rejects; fix = swap in NEW streaming worker (branch streaming-asr/m2-worker-capacity) supporting --max_slots.
root_cause_rediagnosis_vs_task_premise.actual_finding: No streaming-asr/m2-worker-capacity branch and NO binary supporting --max_slots exists in ~/project/repro-qwen3. The bundled qwen3_tts_worker uses getopt_long with NO --max_slots option (parseArgs default:return false -> EXIT_FAILURE on unknown flag). N>1 concurrency is instead driven by the OVS_TTS_WORKER_CONCURRENCY env var (worker has full SlotPool/asyncCode2wavStreams[slot]/dispatcher machinery, readTtsWorkerConcurrencyEnv). The backend (voxedge/backends/jetson/trt_edge_llm_tts.py:807-811) only appends --max_slots when worker_concurrency>1, and at N=1 omits it (which is why qwen3-asr+matcha were coherent). A 2-line overlay patch (drop the --max_slots append, rely on the env var) was prepared and applied to the throwaway container — it is NOT the blocker. The real blockers are the 0.7.1-vs-0.7.0 runtime version mismatch and unified-memory contention above.

blocked_dimensions_investigation.what_was_attempted: Throwaway 'seeed-voice-baseline' on :8631 from :server-loop-prod-v7-asrmultiturn with OVS_PROFILE=jetson-multilang-highperf-nx + HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1, prod host-lib mounts, speech-models volume, CustomVoice engine tree (~/qwen3-tts-export-workspace/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-CustomVoice/engines-nx) mounted at /opt/cv-engines, generic talker+code_predictor backends, and the --max_slots-drop overlay. Service reached 'Application startup complete' (TTS backend=trt_edgellm model_id=qwen3-tts voice_clone) but synthesis produced no audio (blockers 1+2).

hf_artifact_availability_sm87.moss_sm87_PRESENT: models/moss-tts-nano/engines/*.plan (prefill, decode_step, local_decoder, local_cached_step, local_fixed_sampled_frame, *_shared.data) + codec_onnx/*.plan + tokenizer.model + moss_tts_nano_worker + manifest.json. NOTE: task premise 'MOSS failure was no HF bundle for sm87' is OUTDATED — MOSS sm87/trt10.3/jp6.2 engines ARE on HF.
hf_artifact_availability_sm87.other_sm87_bundles: kokoro / matcha / paraformer each have sm87-trt10.3-jp6.2-cuda12.6.tar.gz.

blocked_dimensions_investigation.moss_status: NOT attempted live — same unified-memory contention with edge-llm-chat-service would apply; MOSS engines would need pulling from HF first. MOSS engines DO exist on HF for sm87 (see above), so MOSS is gated by memory contention on this box, not by missing artifacts.
to_complete_baseline: Either (a) capture on a box WITHOUT a co-resident ~10GB LLM (free >=4-5GB unified RAM), OR temporarily stop edge-llm-chat-service (out of scope here), AND

(b) obtain a v0.7.1-coherent qwen3_tts_worker runtime (the available 0.7.0 worker mismatches the 0.7.1 CustomVoice engines) — i.e. rebuild the worker at the 0.7.1 runtime or re-export engines at 0.7.0.
N=2: with a coherent worker, the prepared --max_slots-drop overlay + OVS_TTS_WORKER_CONCURRENCY=2 yields the env-var slot-pool the HTTP-concurrent harness needs.
